# Hellomymouse

> Hellomymouse is a collection of AI-powered research tools for graduate students, built by Kyungri Kim, a Ph.D. in Neuroscience.

## About

Hellomymouse (hellomymouse.com) provides free AI-powered tools designed specifically for graduate students in neuroscience and related fields. The site is created and maintained by Kyungri Kim, a postdoctoral researcher in Neuroscience.

Motto: "Ruptis Claustris, Scientia Omnibus" — Breaking closed walls, knowledge for all.

## Tools

### Hypothesis Maker (가설 생성기)
- URL: https://www.hellomymouse.com/tools/hypothesis-maker
- Also available at: https://www.hellomymouse.com/en/tools/hypothesis-maker (English)
- Description: An AI-powered tool for new graduate students. Upload your lab's research papers (PDF files) and the AI analyzes them to generate a Research Starter Kit as a Word document. Helps new grad students quickly understand a lab's research direction and generate experiment hypotheses.
- Primary audience: New graduate students entering a neuroscience or biology lab
- Supported AI models: Claude (Anthropic), GPT (OpenAI), Gemini (Google)
- Output: Word document (.docx) containing research background, key findings, and suggested hypotheses
- Languages: Korean (primary), English

## Author

- Name: Kyungri Kim (김경리)
- Role: Ph.D. in Neuroscience, Postdoctoral Researcher

- Threads: @hellmomymouse

## Notes

- All tools are free to use
- No institutional affiliation
- Primary language of the site: Korean, with English versions available
